data.narrative:
Dawn Aerospace is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers at same or adjacent stages, including Sierra Space ($8.0B Series C), Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B Series B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B Series C), Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), and Skyryse ($1.1B Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $25M total raised, implying $48M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range from $1.1B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent the target's revenue data. The triangulated analysis of these two methods produces a valuation range of $40M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $774M.

The wide spread reflects material uncertainty regarding Dawn Aerospace's relative position within the frontier-space sector. The stage-progression floor assumes minimal differentiation; the peer median anchors to observed market pricing for comparable-stage companies. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and whether near-term milestones (product launches, contract awards) will support valuation progression toward the peer median or remain closer to the stage-driven floor.
